course_description:
Effective marketing decision making is central to the evaluation, selection, and implementation of marketing strategies. This course places emphasis on the processes of marketing management and on strategic marketing decision making in particular. Further, the course is specifically designed as the capstone course within the Marketing major of the Master of Business Program.

This course focuses on the strategic role of marketing within organisations, both as a business discipline and managerial competence. The course explores key marketing concepts and tools that reflect contemporary marketing theory and practice. It reviews different frameworks that can be used to identify, analyse and solve marketing problems that incorporate both strategic and tactical aspects of marketing management. In doing so, it provides a systematic and integrative approach to marketing analysis, planning and management.ﾠ

course_aims:
The aim of this course is to provide students with a framework for understanding and applying the latest concepts, tools and practices in strategic marketing management.

Specifically, students will:

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the key concepts and tools in strategic marketing management.
Identify the context of an organisation and its marketing philosophy.
Apply an ethical analysis to marketing problems and practices.
Critically evaluate the impact of changing paradigms in marketing.
Identify strategies, tools and metrics for improving marketing effectiveness and market performance.
Working as a group, using Marketing strategy and frameworks to present the groups findings.

assessment_summary.title: Interactive presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: 2/09/2024 - 21/10/2024; On the assigned in-class presentation day.

assessment_details.title: Interactive presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 2/09/2024 - 21/10/2024 On the assigned in-class presentation day.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a group assessment . In this assessment, students in a group setting will critically analyse a case study assigned to them, create a deck of presentation slides, submit the slides to the teaching team prior to their scheduled presentation time, and make an oral presentation based on the slides. These presentations will be scheduled for weekly tutorials. To analyse the case content, students will use marketing strategy concepts and frameworks that will be discussed in lectures. Peer Review : Group members are expected to contribute equally, fulfilling the tasks agreed on. If there are disagreements in relation to team members' contributions that cannot be resolved, students must bring the problem to the attention of their Course Coordinator as early as possible. Students can elect to undertake a Peer Review process, which can influence the marks of those who do not contribute to group work. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
A copy of the presentation slides should be submitted to Blackboard on the specified date.
One student will make the submission on behalf of all team members of the group.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Case study report
assessment_details.task_description: This is an individual assessment . In this assessment, students will critically analyse and evaluate a firm (company) and its market offering based on lecture contents. Broadly, students will: Select a product or product line from a firm of their choice Conduct situational analysis Critically analyse the firm's competitive environment Conduct segmentation analysis Construct a perceptual map that locates the product and competing offerings. Critically evaluate the current positioning of the product and provide feasible and useful suggestions on repositioning strategies. Instructions and marking criteria (rubric) will be provided on Blackboard. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The report is to be submitted to Blackboard on or before the due date.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Online Blackboard exam
assessment_details.task_description: The end-of-semester exam will be essay questions designed to test the depth of knowledge that you have gained from this course and your ability to apply this knowledge to organisations. Some questions may be based on case studies that were presented and discussed in tutorials. This is an online exam. You will receive the specific questions at the start of the exam, and you will submit your answers in a Word document at the end of the exam. Full details, sample questions, and recommended answer strategies will be provided in class. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Lecture 1: Introduction and Course Overview The importance of strategic marketing, overview of the course and assessments. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06

Lecture 2: Strategic Marketing and Environment Strategy v tactics, Marketing strategy process, Core strategy & SWOT analysis Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

Tutorial 2: Strategic Marketing and Environment Group discussion - reflect on lecture 2 content First practice case study for group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 3: Customer and Competitor Analysis Customer analysis: buyer behaviour and market research, Competitor analysis Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Tutorial 3: Customer and Competitor Analysis Group discussion - reflect on lecture 3 contents Second practice case study for group presentation (check blackboard) Note: public holiday on 14/08 (Wednesday); Wednesday students to attend alternative tutorials. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 4: Organisation and Segmentation Organisational resource base, assets and capabilities, segmentation Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05
Tutorial 4: Organisation and Segmentation Group discussion - reflect on lecture 4 contents Third practice case study for group presentation (check Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 5: Targeting and Positioning Segmentation research, Targeting and Positioning Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05

Tutorial 5: Targeting and Positioning Group discussion - reflect on lecture 5 contents Forth (and the last) practice case study for group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 6: Information session for Case Study Report Submission guidelines & marking rubric for the individual case study report Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
Tutorial 6: Information session for Case Study Report Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
Lecture 7: Sustainable Competitive Advantage Sustainable Competitive Advantage Marketing mix - Gaining and sustaining SCA Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
Tutorial 7: Sustainable Competitive Advantage Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 8: Customer management & Sales Approaches to customer management and strategic sales organization Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

Tutorial 8: Customer management & Sales Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Note: public holiday on 07 October (Monday); Alternative presentation schedule for Monday students (check blackboard for updated schedule). Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
Lecture 9: Innovation Innovation strategy New product planning Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
In-Semester Break No classes or tutorials this week
Tutorial 9: Innovation Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05
Lecture 10: Strategic Alliances and Networks Strategic alliances and networks, Implementation and internal marketing Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
Tutorial 10: Strategic Alliances and Networks Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05, L06

Tutorial 11 - Corporate Social Responsibility and Ethics Interactive group presentation (check blackboard) Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06
Lecture 12: Contemporary issues & Course review Strategic marketing in 21st century Course review Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
